entry.title: Remember That Night?
entry.summary:
Author: InterrogateTheCat

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 32,370Chapters: 3/5Kudos: 183

Summary: He was going to watch her find the two seconds of nothingness. And he was going to want it. He was going to need it. He was tired of fighting. He was so unbelievably tired of being strong, of being good, of taking the punishment and hoping that someone, someday, would decide he was worthy of forgiveness, of love. Bobby didn't want him back at the 118. Eddie wouldn't look at him. His parents had never loved him. I wish I could say I feel nothing, Buck thought, a tear finally slipping through his fingers and dropping onto his jeans. But I feel everything. He sat in the darkness of the loft, listening to the shower run, waiting for the ticking clock to strike zero. He was going to relapse tonight. He had already made the choice. Now, it was just a matter of waiting for the moment to arrive. Or, Buck has been sober for six years, during the lawsuit, Buck relapses. 😭🥹💔💊 [You don't need to watch Euphoria to read this, based in the 911 universe, will have enough context about Rue to understand.]

entry.title: Beetles and Elderflowers
entry.summary:
Author: lisaragorn

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 185,938Chapters: 21/30Kudos: 28

Summary: In a reckless act of impulse, Evan Buckley abandons his life in Pennsylvania to follow his sister and what he believes is the love of his life, Abby, to Los Angeles. Enrolling at UCLA, this irresponsible decision becomes the catalyst for an entirely new chapter, one that leads him to cross paths with the enigmatic Eddie Diaz, a young single father struggling to balance his personal life with parenthood. What begins as an unexpected friendship quickly becomes something far more complicated than either of them anticipated.

entry.title: Don’t Give Up On Me
entry.summary:
Author: Anonymous

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 544Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 102

Summary: The bottle rattled as it skidded across the kitchen floor. “Give them to me, Buck.” Eddie’s voice was low, controlled—but just barely. His hand hovered in the air, not quite reaching, like he was afraid of what would happen if he actually touched him. Buck laughed, sharp and hollow. “Or what?” He kicked the bottle farther away, like it was a game. Like this was a game. “You gonna wrestle me for them?” “Don’t do this.” “I’m already doing it.” Buck’s eyes were glassy, too bright, too empty all at once. “You don’t get it, Eddie. I have nothing to live for.”

  • Graphic Depictions Of Violence

entry.title: pillow talk
entry.summary:
Author: madforyou

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 21,069Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 116

Summary: Buck is a lot. Eddie knows Buck is a lot. Buck knows Buck is a lot. But every single person who ever said that to Buck said it like a warning. Like a disclaimer, a heads-up, a you-should-know-what-you're-getting-into before getting too close so they can't say they weren't told. And Eddie has never, not once, wanted Buck to be less. The surplus, the overflow, the fourth tangent on a story that started somewhere else entirely — that's not the price of Buck. That is Buck. And more of Buck has only ever meant more to love, and Eddie spent enough years running on empty to know what it means when someone fills up every room they walk into so completely that Eddie is overflowing with it most days. "Eddie. Are you listening?" Eddie nods. "Gym Katie is alone at the gym and she's upset." Buck blinks. His mouth is still open from the sentence he was about to say, his hands still mid-gesture, and he looks genuinely thrown, like he expected to catch Eddie zoning out and instead got caught himself. Caught being heard. "You were listening," Buck says, a little breathlessly. Eddie smiles. "I'm always listening." Or,Eddie finds the beauty in the mundane.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: The Hermit, the Devil, and the Star
entry.summary:
Author: thetalee

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 95,762Chapters: 28/?Kudos: 432

Summary: Buck is back at the 118, and Eddie's healed from the sniper, but that doesn't mean everything's rainbows and unicorns. He knows there's something wrong with Maddie, no matter how many times she and Chimney say everything's fine. He knows there's something wrong with Eddie, even if his boyfriend insists otherwise. At least everything seems to be okay with Daniel, this time.

entry.title: Who am I to watch you wilt?
entry.summary:
Author: comfortcrowd1122

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 650Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 50

Summary: Eddie knew every version of Buck—the reckless one, the cocky one, the soft one, the terrified one. And this? This was the “I’m fine” act. The one that meant everything was very much not fine. But what could he do? He wanted Buck to willingly open up to him… but this time, he might need a nudge. or Post-episode 9x14. Eddie tries to get Buck to be honest with him.

entry.title: They all say that it gets better (But what if I don't?)
entry.summary:
Author: Buddie_stan

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 12,170Chapters: 2/2Kudos: 179

Summary: Buck never meant for it to get this bad. He didn't even realize when it started to become a problem. First it was just when the pain was really bad. Then it was when he felt sore. He just wanted to stop the pain before it got too bad. But now the pain is gone and yet here he is, popping pills like he’d die if he didn’t. He tried to convince himself that it wasn’t a problem. That it wasn’t as bad as it seems, but the words come out sour. It stains his brain like a raging headache. Then came the lying. And the guilt. And soon enough two pills weren’t enough to numb the ache in his chest. It wasn’t enough to stop the shame he felt building up in his gut. He knew Bobby would be disappointed in him. How could he not? He was disappointed in himself. Everyone thought he was fine. He’d pick out a few smiles he’d wear in the morning and let it play out through the day. It was believable to everyone. Everyone except Eddie. He could see past the fake smiles, and the senseless excuses. He always did. It’s something Buck was always grateful for. Before Buck started lying his ass off, now it just seemed to make him more on edge.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: the man (I thought) I knew
entry.summary:
Author: buddienuts

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,710Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 29

Summary: ” In the time he wasn't spending with Eddie watching over him those first couple of weeks, Buck tried desperately to pick up the scraps of whatever was left of the person he once was. He leaned on past practices and rituals—the tried and true methods for coping and healing—the kitchen. Baking had started out for him as something of a strategy to get his mind off of the things that were bothering him, sprouting in excess after an especially upsetting breakup left him feeling in what he thought was a similar boat. That feeling of not knowing who you are anymore. While it had started out that way, it had transformed itself into something he genuinely enjoyed. Something that made him feel happy. Whatever baking did for him before New Mexico, it didn't have that effect anymore.”Or: Buck was lost in New Mexico, but he was lost before then too, and he’s still lost now.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: Evan Buckley - The Man Who Walked Back Into the Light
entry.summary:
Author: Tigercher11

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,976Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 8

Summary: Buck thought he’d survived the worst night of his life - until the part of him that kept him alive refused to let go.Haunted by Ethan, the ruthless inner protector born from trauma and silence, Buck is barely holding himself together when an unexpected visitor walks back into the firehouse, the man who trained him to endure pain at any cost. What begins as a reunion fractures into a reckoning, forcing Buck to confront not only the voice in his head, but the man who taught him how to bury fear so deep it learned to speak back.

entry.title: Missing the Gun Fights and Coercion
entry.summary:
Author: stupid_for_u

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,580Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 33

Summary: The luck it had taken him to get into the 118, his first station, mind you, had never sat right with him. Then again, lots of things didn’t sit right. Like the fact that on his last shift, he’d nearly been taken out by a supporting beam that the flames hadn’t even reached yet. Logically, Buck knew there was no reason for it to fall, and definitely no reason for it to fall directly at him. In a previous life, Buck would have thought it was planned. In this life, he chalked it up to a freak accident. Those things happened every day; he saw it every day. It was just a freak accident. But then, Edmundo fucking Diaz showed up in his locker room, and Buck knew. He knew someone was trying to kill him. ~~ Buck is a retired spy trying to make his way as a firefighter. His life was going well until his old partner turned up undercover at his station. Eddie was tasked with being Buck's bodyguard now that they were the only two surviving members of their old team. Can they protect each other long enough to figure out who's systematically assassinating everyone from Buck's past life, while simultaneously hiding their connection from the rest of the 118?

entry.title: heaven’s flames
entry.summary:
Author: urineblondie

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 953Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 4

Summary: a church broke down in flames and eddie has to come to terms with his feelings.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
  • Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV)

entry.title: Love isn’t rescue, it’s someone to lose
entry.summary:
Author: Whatdawnknows

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,691Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 67

Summary: In his front pocket, his phone buzzes. Now he remembers. He remembers saying goodbye to Eddie and Chris, watching them drive away at last with a shaky sigh, the guilt twisting in his gut that he was relieved to see them go. Stumbling over here to shake out some pills, probably the right number. Sinking down onto this bench because he couldn’t bear the weight of his thoughts for one more second. He must have fallen asleep. The meds can cause drowsiness, but it’s more than that. Sometimes once the pain, the stress, the panic is gone, the bone-deep weariness is all that’s left. Pretending to be okay all the time takes a toll. He hadn’t dreamed, thank God.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
  • Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV)
  • Evan "Buck" Buckley & Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV)
  • Evan "Buck" Buckley

  • Pre-Relationship
  • Evan "Buck" Buckley Needs A Hug

  • But Eddie saves him dw

entry.title: in perfect symmetry
entry.summary:
Author: ari_kart

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,136Chapters: 2/2Kudos: 1,270

Summary: There is something so glaringly not Buck about the scene in front of him. That knot of dread becomes a boulder, plunging into Eddie’s gut and nearly knocking the wind out of him. “Hey, Chris, wait here a second, would you?” Eddie’s already crossing the threshold. “But, it’s just Buck-” “Chris,” Eddie turns around, scrambling to collect the worries that are slipping through his internal defenses like a sieve. He meets Christopher’s gaze, his son no longer a boy but a young man. He doesn’t ask or demand. He begs. “Please. Please just wait.” It’s probably fine. All a misunderstanding. Eddie is jumping to conclusions. Even at his worst, Buck would never—but if Eddie’s worries are well-founded, he would never forgive himself for letting Chris see what’s inside that house. -Or, Eddie uses his key to Buck's house and finds himself in a situation that is all too familiar, only this time he's on the other side of the door. Meanwhile Buck struggles to cope with eight years of love and loss, oh, and that whole kidnapping thing.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
  • Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV)

entry.title: Dean Begins (Again)
entry.summary:
Author: redwinesupernatural

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 31,051Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 188

Summary: Chuck was gone, the world was safe, and Jack was the new God of this joint.Dean Winchester decided to use his new found freedom of responsibilities to pack up and move to Los Angeles to stick his feet in the sand, get some sun, enjoy the movie magic in motion, and decide how he wants to spend the rest of his life. He was definitely not running away from the personal consequences and feelings of the past few months.At least, that is what he had thought at first.He did not expect to be peer pressured into attending Alcoholics Anonymous by Sam, Jody, and by extension the wayward sisters and Eileen, when they decided that Dean needed to stop with the liquor and spirits, and instead deal with the ghosts of grief weighing down his shoulders.He also didn't expect to be taken under the wing of a firefighting captain with the same name as one of the ghosts he was running from.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
